$comment: The Gateway Corp vetted tool catalog. At most ONE approved entry per slot per repo. Entries with defaultInstall:true are installed by org-init in every repo unless skipWhen matches or the user opts out. Changes land via PR reviewed by the DRI. Versions are pinned - re-vet on major bumps.

slots.memory: Cross-session memory. Default occupant: claude-mem (installed at init). Exactly one memory system per repo - native auto-memory is disabled when claude-mem is active.
slots.code-graph: Persistent structural index of the codebase. Default occupant: codegraph; graphify instead for docs/SQL/mixed-media corpora. Never both.
slots.search: Semantic code + web search. Default occupant: mgrep (replaces grep/rg in workflows; --web covers web search).
slots.editing-precision: Symbol-level navigation and surgical edits via language servers. Default: none (opt-in).
slots.stack-conventions: Per-stack rules, reviewers, and conventions. Filled per repo by cherry-picking from vetted libraries (ECC) via find-org-skills - not by always-on stack plugins.
slots.method: Workflow discipline. Always occupied by gateway-core; no alternatives.
slots.library: Vetted cherry-pick sources. Not installable as bundles.

entries.skipWhen: Machine cannot run background workers (no Bun/uv) or repo handles regulated data without <private> tagging discipline
entries.summary: Exhaustive session memory: lifecycle hooks capture every tool call, background worker compresses into searchable SQLite + vector store, digest injected at SessionStart. The org's default memory layer.

config.required: Disable native auto-memory (autoMemoryEnabled: false) - running both causes documented drift and prompt bloat. Set CONTEXT_SESSIONS=5 to cap startup injection.
config.note: Compression consumes model usage; SessionStart injection is a per-session token tax. gateway-core deliberately ships no memory hooks of its own - claude-mem owns this slot.

entries.cautions: Fast-moving single-lead project - pin, re-vet on majors. Needs Bun + uv + a background worker; use <private> tags around sensitive content.

entries.skipWhen: graphify selected instead (docs/SQL/mixed-media-heavy corpus), or repo is tiny throwaway work
entries.summary: Tree-sitter AST -> SQLite call/import graph per repo. One listed MCP tool (codegraph_explore), near-zero context overhead. Repo-median benchmarks: ~58% fewer tool calls, 23-64% fewer tokens vs native Explore. Value grows with repo size; the incremental watcher keeps a graph started early cheap to maintain.
entries.cautions: Single maintainer; AST-only (no runtime truth); npm path only, never curl|sh; on very high-churn monorepos sync cost can eat savings - flag in harness.json if so.

entries.summary: Knowledge graph over mixed corpora - code plus SQL, docs, PDFs, diagrams - with a PreToolUse hook steering Claude to consult the graph before searching files. Choose over codegraph when the corpus is genuinely mixed-media.
install: then run /graphify . once in Claude Code

config.refreshPolicy: Run the bundled incremental update flow after significant changes; a stale graph misdirects the agent.

entries.cautions: PyPI package is graphifyy (double y). Pre-1.0, near-daily releases. Code-only builds are free/local; doc extraction costs tokens at build time. Token-savings claims (49-71x) are vendor-reported.

entries.summary: Semantic search by mixedbread-ai replacing grep/rg: `mgrep "query"` for local code search, `mgrep --web "query"` for web search. ECC's 50-task benchmark: ~2x fewer tokens than grep-based workflows at similar or better quality.

config.note: Workflows and agents should prefer mgrep over grep/rg; the marketplace is allowlisted in managed settings.

entries.cautions: Third-party marketplace - pinned in managed settings allowlist; DRI re-vets on updates. Verify license terms before org-wide rollout.

entries.summary: Everything Claude Code (affaan-m/ECC, MIT): 278 skills, 67 agents, rules for most stacks (Django/Spring/Laravel/Go/Rust/TS...), reviewers and build resolvers per language. The org's approved source for per-stack conventions and specialist agents - individual files copied, adapted, and pinned via find-org-skills proposals.

config.rule: NEVER install the ecc plugin bundle (/plugin install ecc@ecc is banned - context bloat + large auto-executing surface). Cherry-pick single files, strip hooks/auto-update/npx-launches, pin the source commit, keep MIT attribution.

entries.cautions: Third-party audit flagged the full bundle's auto-executing surface; a malware clone circulated (arabicapp/everything-claude-code) - only ever pull from the pinned official repo.

entries.summary: LSP-backed semantic retrieval plus symbolic editing (replace a function body, insert respecting scope) across 40+ languages. Complements codegraph: graph for discovery, LSP for surgical edits.
install: Follow the repo README; pin the release the DRI vetted.

config.note: Heavier runtime (spawns language servers), larger MCP tool surface - check /context cost after enabling.

entries.cautions: Enable only where symbol-level editing is a real bottleneck; consider native LSP plugins first.

entries.summary: Claude Code built-in per-project memory (MEMORY.md index, on by default since v2.1.59). The fallback memory occupant when claude-mem is skipped (locked-down machines, regulated data).

proposalProcess: New entries arrive as PRs with status 'proposed' (see the find-org-skills skill for required fields). The DRI reviews within 2 working days: license, maintenance signals, executed surface (hooks/MCP/scripts), context cost, slot conflicts - then flips to 'approved' or closes with a reason. AgentShield CI scans every catalog change.
